title: SignWell Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**SignWell** is a transactional e-signature platform exposing a REST API under `https://www.signwell.com/api/v1`. This connector — `SignWellConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "signwell"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Account | `/me` | Authenticated account metadata + plan quotas |
| Documents | `/documents/*` | Create, send, list, get, cancel, archive, delete, download signed PDF |
| Document Templates | `/document_templates/documents` | Instantiate a document from a reusable template |
| Templates | `/templates/*` | List + read reusable templates |
| Recipients / Reminders | `/documents/{id}/recipients[/...]` | List recipients, send per-recipient reminder |
| Webhooks | `/api_application/webhooks` | Register + list + delete webhook subscriptions |

The connector normalises documents into `NormalizedDocument` with **tenant-scoped ids** (`id = f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces every user operation as a standalone `async def` method (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::SignWellHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a SignWell API key

1. Sign in to https://www.signwell.com.
2. Open **Settings → API → API Keys**.
3. Click **Generate API Key**, label it (e.g. `shielva-prod`), and copy the value — SignWell shows it only once.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → SignWell**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_key` (secret) | API key from step 3 |
| `test_mode_default` (boolean) | `true` for dev/test, `false` for production |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://www.signwell.com/api/v1` (rarely needed) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 100 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `api_key` is present, persists non-secret config, and materialises a `TokenInfo` for surface compatibility. It does NOT call the SignWell API — the gateway's first `health_check()` does that.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which hits `GET /me` as a lightweight probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `OFFLINE + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with a structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
SignWell REST API uses **header-based API key authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no refresh, no expiry — the key stays valid until you revoke it.

### Credential model

The API key is sent in the `X-Api-Key` header — **NOT** `Authorization: Bearer …` (a common SignWell-vs-everyone-else gotcha). Sending the key in `Authorization` returns 401.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_key`, persists non-secret config, materialises a TokenInfo. Does **not** call the SignWell API. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the api_key (surface compatibility — no exchange). |
| `health_check()` | `GET /me` — minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | The api_key is materialised once into a TokenInfo at install time. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
X-Api-Key:    <api_key>
Accept:       application/json    (or application/pdf on completed_pdf download)
Content-Type: application/json    (when a body is present)
User-Agent:   shielva-signwell-connector/1.0
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_key` | secret | yes | `self.api_key` | `X-Api-Key` header value |
| `test_mode_default` | boolean | no | `self.test_mode_default` (default `true`) | Default `test_mode` for created documents |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://www.signwell.com/api/v1`) | Override for sandbox / proxy |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 100) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_key"]
OPTIONAL_CONFIG_KEYS = ["test_mode_default", "base_url", "rate_limit_per_min"]

_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_SIGNWELL_BASE = "https://www.signwell.com/api/v1"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `SignWellConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::SignWellHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_key`, persist non-secret config, materialise a `TokenInfo` for surface compatibility. Does NOT call the SignWell API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a TokenInfo whose `access_token` is the configured api_key.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
`GET /me`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Pages through `/documents`, normalises each entry → `NormalizedDocument` (tenant-scoped id), and ingests. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Account
children.content:
### `async get_me() -> Dict`
`GET /me` — returns the authenticated SignWell account payload (id, email, plan, quota counters).

children.title: Documents
children.content:
### `async list_documents(page=1, status=None, archived=False, q=None) -> Dict`
`GET /documents?page={N}&status={S}&archived={A}&q={Q}`. Pagination is page-based; SignWell returns `next_page` / `has_more` to indicate continuation.

### `async get_document(document_id) -> Dict`
`GET /documents/{id}`.

### `async create_document(name, recipients, files=None, file_urls=None, message=None, subject=None, test_mode=None, draft=False, embedded_signing=False, expires_in=None, reminders=True) -> Dict`
`POST /documents`. Validates `recipients` is non-empty with `name`+`email` per entry, and that `files` OR `file_urls` is provided. `test_mode` defaults to the install-time `test_mode_default`.

### `async send_document(document_id) -> Dict`
`POST /documents/{id}/send` — release a draft.

### `async cancel_document(document_id) -> Dict`
`POST /documents/{id}/cancel`.

### `async archive_document(document_id) -> Dict`
`POST /documents/{id}/archive`.

### `async delete_document(document_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /documents/{id}`.

### `async download_completed_document(document_id, type='completed') -> bytes`
`GET /documents/{id}/completed_pdf` — raw PDF bytes. Alias: `download_document` (kept for back-compat).

children.title: Templates
children.content:
### `async list_templates(page=1, q=None) -> Dict`
`GET /templates`.

### `async get_template(template_id) -> Dict`
`GET /templates/{id}`.

### `async create_document_from_template(template_id, name, recipients, template_fields=None, test_mode=None, draft=False, embedded_signing=False) -> Dict`
`POST /document_templates/documents`. `template_fields` is a list of `{api_id, value}` pairs to pre-fill template fields.

children.title: Recipients & Reminders
children.content:
### `async list_recipients(document_id) -> Dict`
`GET /documents/{id}/recipients`.

### `async send_reminder(document_id, recipient_id) -> Dict`
`POST /documents/{did}/recipients/{rid}/reminder` — send a per-recipient reminder email.

children.title: Webhooks
children.content:
### `async list_webhooks() -> Dict`
`GET /api_application/webhooks`.

### `async create_webhook(url, events=None) -> Dict`
`POST /api_application/webhooks` with body `{url, events}`. `events` filters which SignWell events trigger the webhook (e.g. `document_completed`, `document_signed`).

### `async delete_webhook(webhook_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /api_application/webhooks/{id}`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
SignWellError                              # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── SignWellAuthError                      # 401 / 403
├── SignWellBadRequestError                # 400
├── SignWellNotFoundError                  # 404
├── SignWellConflictError                  # 409 (cancel-on-completed, etc.)
├── SignWellRateLimitError                 # 429 — retry_after_s
├── SignWellServerError                    # 5xx
└── SignWellNetworkError                   # transport (timeout / DNS / reset)
```

Back-compat aliases:
```
SignWellNotFound = SignWellNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `0.5s, 1s, 2s`, retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3`. Honours `Retry-After` header when present. |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.TransportError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `SignWellNetworkError` |

`helpers/utils.py::with_retry` adds a higher-level retry layer for transient errors that escape the client (e.g. JSON-decode flakiness on intermittent proxies).

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches typed exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
5xx / transport → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_key` blank | Generate a new key in SignWell → Settings → API → API Keys; paste it into ACP. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — API key invalid or revoked | Verify the key was copied verbatim (no leading/trailing whitespace). Generate a new key if needed. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 — account suspended or key scope missing | Check billing + scope in SignWell; re-enable or rotate the key. |
| Sending the key in `Authorization: Bearer …` returns 401 | SignWell-specific: uses `X-Api-Key`, not `Authorization` | The connector already does this — only relevant when wiring a raw HTTP call outside the connector. |
| `signwell.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (per-minute cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff and honours `Retry-After`. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` install field or batch fewer concurrent requests. |
| `recipients[N].email is required` ValueError | Recipient missing required field | Every recipient must be `{name: '...', email: '...'}` at minimum. |
| `at least one of files or file_urls is required` ValueError | `create_document` called without document content | Provide `files=[{name, file_base64}, ...]` or `file_urls=['https://...']`. |
| 404 on `download_completed_document` | Document not yet completed | The completed_pdf path is only available once the document status is `completed`. Poll `get_document` first. |
| Webhook events not arriving | Webhook URL not reachable / signature mismatch | Verify the URL is publicly reachable; SignWell signs webhooks with the `X-SignWell-Signature` header — verify on the receiving side. |
